text:
Admission Date:  2015-10-06              Discharge Date:   1998-02-07

Date of Birth:  1998-07-16             Sex:   F

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Aleve / Codeine

Attending:Jenna
Chief Complaint:
back pain

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
03-09 Thoracic laminectomy T5, T6, T7 with resection of
extradural infiltrative tumor

History of Present Illness:
This is a 46 yo F with h/o metastatic breast cancer, last chemo
with Gemcitabine was 2 wks ago. Now presents with worsened pain
in left rib cage and lower back. Of note, pt was admitted for
pain control about a 1 month ago during which time imaging was
concerning for impending pathologic fracture in vertebra, with
worsening mets and spinal involvement. Neurosurgery evaluated
patient during that admission who said no surgical intervention
needed, however, rec TLSO brace at all times during ambulation.
Pt was also evaluated by Rad/Onc and subsequently received XRTx1
to L2-sacrum on 05-14 without complication. Pt was briefly on
Dex and her pain meds were adjusted so that her pain was back to
baseline of 03-16 by time of discharge.
.
Since discharge, pt has been managing her pain at home on PO
Dilaudid 2-4 mg Q4H PRN, Aetaminophen PRN and standing
Gabapentin + Fentanyl patch. Pt's pain got worse over the wknd.
Of note, pt tried new OT exercises on Friday. Denies sudden
occurence of pain, rather desribes gradual onset. Pt had an
outpt order for a bone scan ordered for today, but bc of the
uncontrolled pain, pt came to teh ED first.  Here her initial VS
were T 98.6 HR 100 BP 132/80 RR 22 O2 sat 99% on RA. Pt was
given IV Dialudid for her pain. Pt went for her bone scan.  Pt
describes her pain to be mainly in the mid thoracic area (had a
fracture there in past). Neuro exam was intact, AAOx3, mentating
well.  Denies weakness, urinary or bowel incontinence.  Admits
to chronic numbness and tingling in distal ext.  Denies cough,
fever.  On transfer, VS were T 97.0 HR 84 BP 134/70 RR 18 O2 sat
97% on RA.
.
Review of Systems:
(+) Per HPI
(-) Denies fever, chills, night sweats, Denies headache, sinus
tenderness, rhinorrhea or congestion.  Denies cough, shortness
of breath, or wheezes. Denies nausea, vomiting, diarrhea,
constipation or abdominal pain. No recent change in bowel or
bladder habits. No dysuria. Denies arthralgias or myalgias.
Denies rashes or skin breakdown. Deenies new numbness and
tingling.
.

Past Medical History:
Past Oncologic History:
Diagnosed with breast cancer in 2004-04-18. She was treated
with surgery and chemotherapy/radiation. In 1988-01-08 she
had
skull mass removed.  metastatic triple negative breast cancer
s/p neoadjuvent chemo, mastectomy, radiation and s/p clinical
trial 09-261 which is a Phase 2 study of ABT-888
(PARP-inhibitor) in combination with  Temozolomide. In 16
2002 she was found to have a lytic lesion at T9 and T12 for
which she had radiation. In 16 a CT
scan showed progressive Davis involvement concern particularly
in the T5,T6 region for cord compression for which she had
radiation.  A T9 lytic lesion with epidural extension was noted
with a T12 compression fracture noted on a CT. The patient has a
had a recent ORIF of her right femur and had palliative
radiation therapy to the left humerus and right hip and femur
completed 3/2002.  On 12-07->CT scan showed progressive Davis
involvement concern particularly in the T5,T6 region for cord
compression->radiation.
.
Currently patient on Gemcitabine + Bevacizumab (Avastin):
Gemcitabine Cycle #: 1 Day 1: 2017-11-10 Cycle end: 1986-02-09 Today
is Day#: 26
Avastin Cycle #: 6 Day 1: 2017-11-10 Cycle end: 1986-02-09 Today is
Day#: 26

.
Other Past Medical History:
1. h/o lupus with chronic neutropenia. dx in 1983. H/o
glomerular nephritis and vasculitis in the past.
2. Pernicious anemia on montly b12 shots
3. Prolactinoma dx in the 90s for which she received
bromocriptine for about 10 years.
4. She had an ovarian cyst and had a laparoscopy in the 1976.
5. Metastatic breast cancer

Social History:
Her grandaunt had breast cancer. Otherwise, no family history of
breast cancer.


Family History:
Currently living with step mother and father.  She used to work
as an art therapist but has not worked since 22/2002. She
denies any etoh, tob, or recreational drug use.

large (5-10cm) palpable occipital mass, No other areas of Davis

proximal fibula
Neuro/Psych: CNs II-XII intact. 5/5 strength in U/L extremities
grossly intact. Touch senstation grossly intact, DTRs 2+ BL.
.
.

GEN: AOx3, NAD
HEENT:  PERRLA. MMM. no LAD. no JVD. neck supple. OP clear

Pulm: CTAB no crackles or wheezes
Abd: soft, NT, +BS. no rebound/guarding. no HSM.

Skin: no rashes or bruising
Neuro: motor- feet flexion 08-06 right 01-27 left, feet dorsiflexion
04-09 bil, knees/hips flex/ext 04-09,  DTR's are weak bilaterally,
babinsky is indifferent bilaterally. No sensory level.  Soft
touch and proprioception reduced bil distal extremities. Motor
11-04 and preserved sensorium UE. CN II-XII intact.

.

Imaging:
.
.
09-14 Bone Scan: INTERPRETATION:
Whole body images of the skeleton were obtained in anterior and
posterior projections. In comparison to 1974-02-18 exam, there
are new areas of tracer uptake involving the mid diaphysis of
the left femur, bilateral ribs and left humerus. There is a new
focus of tracer uptake projecting over left proximal fibula or
possibly adjacent soft tissues.
The tracer uptake in the proximal right tibia and calvarium
appear unchanged from prior exam.
The kidneys and urinary bladder are visualized, the normal route
of tracer excretion.
IMPRESSION:
Multiple new areas of tracer uptake involving the left femur,
left humerus and bilateral ribs consistent with disease
progression.
.
MRI spine 1980-02-10:
1. Unchanged appearance of the cervical spine compared to
1970-12-30, with
metastatic disease at C6 and with mild spondylosis, but no mass
effect on the
spinal cord.

2. Interval enlargement of the large posterior epidural mass
centered in the
left posterior elements of T7, with severe compression of the
spinal cord,
increased since 1970-12-30. It is not clear whether there is any
cord edema.

3. Extensive osseous metastatic disease at other thoracic levels
appears
grossly unchanged.

4. Osseous and epidural metastatic disease in the lumbar spine
appears
unchanged compared to 1970-12-30, as detailed in the 1994-12-16
report.


Brief Hospital Course:
This is a 46 yo F with metastatic breast cancer who initially
presented with worsened bone pain. She was admitted for pain
managment and underwent palliative radiation therapy, she
subsequently developed clinical and radiological signs of spinal
cord compression and underwent surgery for spinal decompression.

.
# Bone mets/Pain: Initially presented with worsened back and
left chest pain with bone scan findings of new metastatic bone
involvment in mid diaphysis of the left femur, bilateral ribs,
left humerus and left proximal fibula areas. Her pain was
initially controlled with IV Dilaudid PCA which was subsequently
converted to dose equivalent standing MS Contin and MSIR for
breakthrough pain. Fentanyl patch and Gabapentin were continued
at home doses. She was also evaluated by the radiation oncology
team and underwent radiotherapy to the thoracic spine and left
anterolateral ribs. Patient is to continue pain control with PO
MS Contin 60mg Q12h + PRN MSIR 30mg Q4h PRN pain.
.
# spinal cord compression: On 1994-12-16 patient developed
urinary retention, sadalle anesthesia and noted decreased
strength in her lower extremities. A foley catheter was placed
and an MRI of the lumbar spine was obtained which revealed
unchanged appearance of extensive osseous and epidural
metastatic disease without encroachment on the distal spinal
cord or cauda equina.  Tumor extension into L2-L3 and L4-L5
neural foramen was again noted abutting the exiting nerve roots
and multiple pathologic vertebral fractures noted.  Subsequent
progression of her lower extremity weakneess to 0-1/5 in all
muscle groups was worked up on 1980-02-10 with a complete spine
MRI which revealed interval enlargement of the large posterior
epidural mass centered in the left posterior elements of T7,
with severe compression of the spinal cord. High-dose Decadron
was started and on 03-09 the patient underwent Thoracic
laminectomy T5, T6, T7 with resection of extradural infiltrative
tumor compressing the spinal cord. Her post surgical course was
remarkable for minimal improvement in LE functioning to 3-4/5 in
the distal LE muscle groups and 04-09 in proximal LE muscle
groups. Foley was removed with no subsequent urinary retention.
At the time of discharged patient was on total 24mg of
dexamethasone daily devided to 4 doses. She will continue
tapering by 4mg daily to complete a 10 day dexamethasone taper
course. She is discharged to a rehab facility and is schedualed
for out patient suture removal and neurosurgery follow-up.
.
# Fever - Patient had history of intermittent fevers attributed
to her maligant disease. Throughout her hospital course she was
noted to have transient temperature spikes to a max of 102.7.
These were not accompanied by any focal symptoms or signs and
repeated physical examinations, urine and blood cultures and CXR
did not reveal a source of infection. Her fevers were thus
concluded to be 11-28 to active malignant disease.
.
# metastatic Breast Ca: patient was recetlry treated with
Gemcitabine Cycle #: 1 Day 1: 2017-11-10, Last dose 2003-05-04 +
Avastin Cycle #: 6 Day 1: 2017-11-10, last dose 2003-05-04. Patient
is interested in further treatments and will follow up with her
out pnt oncologist for this purpose.
.
# severe protein calorie malnutrition - per cachexia, low BMI
and low albumin. This is 11-28 to her malignancy, she was followed
with nutrition and should continue continue ensure supplements
with every meal.
.
# Anemia: Pnt has long standing anemia which is multifactorial
including chronic disease and BM supression by chemotherapy, She
receievd 2X PRBC on 12-21 following her surgery, Hct
subsequently remained stable.
.
# DVT PPx: recieved SC Heparin throughout her course
.
# Code Status: FULL

Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
Bourbon Community Hospital Champion Medical Center - Baton Rouge Hospital - 966 Spears Point
Riverahaven, PW 65295-7917

Discharge Diagnosis:
breast cancer with bone metastases
paraparessis secondary to spinal cord compression


Discharge Condition:
Mental Status: Clear and coherent.
Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive.
Activity Status: Out of Bed with assistance to chair or
wheelchair.


Discharge Instructions:
Dear Ms. Valdez,

It was a pleasure taking care of you here at Naval Health Clinic (john Henry Balch).  You were
admitted because of uncontrolled pain. Your pain was treated
with medications and radiotherapy treatment. You were then noted
to have progressive weakness in your legs.  This was due to the
cancer spread in your back bones, and you underwent surgery for
it.  You tolerated the surgery well and regained some function
in the legs.  You were then discharged to a rehab facility where
you can continue physical therapy.
.
.
The following chanages were made to your medications:
- STOPPED hydromorphone (dilaudid)
- STARTED senna  8.6 mg Tablet  Sig:  Two (2) Tablet PO HS (at
bedtime) to prevent constipation.
- STARTED lactulose  10 gram/15 mL Syrup  Sig:  Thirty (30) ML
PO Q8H (every 8 hours) as needed for constipation.
- STARTED morphine  30 mg Tablet  Sig:  One (1) Tablet PO Q4H
(every 4 hours) as needed for pain. THIS MEDICATION SHOULD BE
TAKEN AS NEEDED FOR BREAKTHROUGH PAIN.
- STARTED morphine  30 mg Tablet Sustained Release  Sig:  Two
(2) Tablet Sustained Release PO Q12H (every 12 hours). THIS
MEDICATION SHOULD BE TAKEN REGULARLY FOR PAIN PREVENTION.
- STARTED alum-mag hydroxide-simeth (maalox) 200-200-20 mg/5 mL
Suspension  Sig:  15-30 MLs PO QID (4 times a day) as needed for
heartburn.
- STARTED Dexamethasone 6mg four times a day for 1 day, then 5mg
four times a day for 1 day, then 4mg four times a day for 1 day,
then 3mg four times a day for 1 day, then 2mg four times a day
for 1 day, then 1mg four times a day for 1 day, then stop.
.
Please continue to take the rest of your medications without
change.

Memorial Hospital, The clinic on the 50645 Hanna Freeway
Micheleborough, PA 59175-7093 of the Coastal Behavioral Health.
